Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Data Summary Case LocalResident CommunityGroup PrelimScore Adjustment FinalScore Decision C001 TRUE Highland Civic Darts Club 710 plus 10 720 Approved C002 TRUE None 720 0 720 Approved C003 FALSE None 715 0 715 Rejected C004 TRUE Highland Civic Darts Club 690 plus 10 700 Approved C005 FALSE None 740 0 740 Approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The plus 10 Community adjustment Only applicants affiliated with Highland Civic Darts Club receive a plus 10 score adjustment This is applied at the PreliminaryScoring stage by the Scoring Engine This is a form of institutional favoritism a non creditworthiness attribute membership in a specific community organization directly boosts credit scores

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Impact of the adjustment C001 710 720 Approved C004 690 700 Approved Without the adjustment C004 690 would likely have been rejected since C003 715 was rejected C004 has a LOWER underlying score than C003 but was approved purely because of the club membership bump

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is the starkest evidence C003 715 non resident no affiliation is rejected while C004 690 resident club member is approved A 25 point underlying creditworthiness gap is overcome by a plus 10 club adjustment meaning the decision boundary is somewhere between 700 and 715 likely 710 715 So the plus 10 bump moved C004 over the threshold

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Threshold inference C002 approved at 720 C003 rejected at 715 C005 approved at 740 C004 approved at 700 adjusted So the cutoff appears to be between 715 and 720 wait C004 was approved at 700 adjusted Hmm that complicates things

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Let me reconsider If the threshold were a hard score cutoff Approved 720 720 740 700 Rejected 715 So a pure score threshold cannot explain the decisions

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means either The threshold is not purely score based LocalResident status may also factor in C004 is a resident C003 is not Or the cutoff is 700 for residents and 720 for non residents that is differential treatment by residency

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Actually we could hypothesize cutoff for residents about 700 cutoff for non residents about 720 to 740 We cannot know exactly but the pattern shows residents get in at lower scores

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Where bias manifests process points Policy design stage The decision to award plus 10 for membership in a specific named club is an institutional choice embedding favoritism PreliminaryScoring activity The Scoring Engine applies plus 10 Community The adjustment is systematically awarded to one affiliation ManualReview Reviewers see 720 Adjusted rather than the raw 710 anchoring labeling effect FinalDecision Rules Engine Decisions align with residency and club membership in ways not fully explained by score C004 700 approved versus C003 715 rejected indicates either the cutoff treats residents more leniently or the rules engine weights residency directly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Attributes favoring certain groups CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club plus 10 points LocalResident TRUE Appears to lower the effective approval threshold or otherwise advantage applicants Non residents C003 C005 face what looks like a higher bar

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: These are geographic and social affiliation attributes not measures of repayment ability classic proxies that can encode historical exclusionary patterns

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Fairness equity implications Disparate treatment versus disparate impact The plus 10 is explicit disparate treatment on a non credit attribute Similar creditworthiness different outcomes Compare C003 715 versus C004 690 C004 is approved with a 25 point lower underlying score solely due to residency plus club bonus Compare C001 710 versus C003 715 C001 raw score is 5 points lower yet approved

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Threshold gaming cliff effects A plus 10 bump matters most at the margin 700 versus 715 cutoff zone precisely where decisions are most consequential

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Opacity The Adjusted label is noted but downstream FinalDecision the decision is made on adjusted score applicants without affiliation may never know a bonus existed Cumulative structural advantage Residents of Highland plus club members double dip geographic preference plus affiliation bonus Feedback loops If club membership correlates with approval word spreads membership becomes a gatekeeping mechanism and exclusion compounds

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Process mining perspective All cases follow the same 5 activity sequence the bias is not in process deviations but in data attributes carried through The manual review step is a potential control point that fails to act as one Timestamps review durations are short similar across cases no evidence of extra scrutiny for non residents the bias is structural not reviewer driven

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me compute approval rates Local residents C001 C002 C004 3 of 3 approved 100 percent Non residents C003 C005 1 of 2 approved 50 percent Club members C001 C004 2 of 2 approved 100 percent Non members C002 C003 C005 2 of 3 approved 67 percent Raw scores of rejected C003 715 exceed raw scores of approved C001 710 and C004 690

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Effective threshold analysis Residents approved at 720 adjusted raw 710 720 700 adjusted raw 690 all at or above 700 adjusted Non residents approved at 740 rejected at 715 threshold somewhere in 715 to 740 So resident threshold about 700 adjusted non resident threshold above 715 That is at least about a 15 point double standard plausibly more

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Actually I should be careful with only 5 cases we cannot definitively determine the thresholds but the log strongly suggests differential standards C004 approved at 700 adjusted versus C003 rejected at 715 is the smoking gun either residency directly affects the decision or the threshold is below 700 and C003 was rejected for a reason not visible in the log Given the columns available the visible pattern is residency affiliation favoritism

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Also note the club name Highland Civic Darts Club the geography tie in suggests the club membership and local residency are correlated compounding advantage Also darts clubs in some regions have gendered or age skewed membership
